creator: david@catwhisker.org
text:
On Fri, Feb 07, 2014 at 09:54:17AM +0900, Ken DEGUCHI wrote:
> I think that "OPTIONS_DEFINE=..." etc. are in front of ".include <bsd.port.options.mk>".
> 
> Attached patch probably fix this problem.
> ....

That's a definite improvement, but at least some issue(s) remain.

I have 2 drives, each of which has 4 bootable slices:

* amd64 WITH_NEW_XORG
* i386 WITHOUT_NEW_XORG

After the patch, the amd64/NEW_XORG works fine: I no longer see the
"signal 22" and hang for stable/10 or head; I am able to build
x11/nvidia-driver as part of my kernel builds again (by specifying
"PORTS_MODULES=x11/nvidia-driver" in /etc/src.conf) for stable/9,
stable/10, and head.

For the i386/WITHOUT_NEW_XORG, I've only tested an attempted "portmaster
x11/nvidia-driver" while running stable/9 @r261539 (ports tree @r343033
-- plus the patch, of course).  And that failed as before:

g1-251(9.2-S)[9] sudo svn patch  /tmp/patch.txt
U         Makefile
g1-251(9.2-S)[10] ls
Makefile        distinfo        files           pkg-descr       pkg-plist
